Islamic State.description: Transnational Salafi jihadist militant organization and unrecognized quasi-state that proclaimed a worldwide caliphate in 2014. Originating from al-Qaeda in Iraq, it controlled large territories in Iraq and Syria from 2013 to 2019, enforcing extremist Islamic law. Known for brutal violence, including genocides, beheadings, and terrorist attacks globally. After losing its Middle Eastern territories, it reverted to insurgency and maintains significant presence in Africa.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf pledged allegiance to Islamic State, becoming its East Asia Province.
edges.evidence_quote: On July 23, 2014, Isnilon Hapilon, one of the group's leaders, swore an oath of loyalty to Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi, the leader of the Islamic State (IS) officially aligning the group with the global jihadist movement.

descriptions: Islamic State is listed as an opponent in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Islamic State

descriptions: ISIL killed an Ahrar al-Sham commander, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 2013, members of ISIL killed the Ahrar al-Sham commander Abu Obeida Al-Binnishi, after he had intervened to protect a Malaysian Islamic charity; ISIL had mistaken its Malaysian flag for that of the United States.

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham and ISIL became hostile in February 2014.
edges.evidence_quote: Ahrar al-Sham had worked with ISIL until the two groups began their present-day hostilities with one another in February 2014.

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham cooperated with ISIL in some battles but disagreed on agenda.
edges.evidence_quote: Ahrar worked with the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) in some battles, but that their agenda was disagreeable.

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham cooperated with ISIL until they became hostile in February 2014.
edges.evidence_quote: Ahrar al-Sham had worked with ISIL until the two groups began their present-day hostilities with one another in February 2014.

descriptions: Ajang Ajang group's bombs carried the signature of Islamic State, indicating affiliation.
edges.evidence_quote: The two bombs carried the signature of the Ajang Ajang's elders, Islamic State.

descriptions: Al-Nusra and ISIL were rival jihadist groups.
edges.evidence_quote: The tactics of Al-Nusra Front differed markedly from those of its rival jihadist group ISIL

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed the Islamic State.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Islamic State

descriptions: Al-Nusra and ISIL engaged in open armed conflict after a dispute over merger.
edges.evidence_quote: Open fighting between al-Nusra and ISIL (2013–2015)

descriptions: ISIS and Al-Nusra conducted a joint operation to capture Lebanese soldiers and police.
edges.evidence_quote: a joint ISIS–al-Nusra operation along with 29 civilians, some of whom are known terrorists.

descriptions: ISIL seceded from al-Qaeda and became an opponent.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda in Iraq (became the Islamic State of Iraq, which later seceded from al-Qaeda and became ISIL)

descriptions: ISIL and al-Qaeda were former allies but later became divided.
edges.evidence_quote: condemned al-Zawahiri for not making this same claim for Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi. Al-Zawahiri was encouraging factionalism and division between former allies of ISIL such as the Al-Nusra Front.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda affiliated branches joined forces with ISIS in Libya.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 2017, spokesman of the Libyan National Army (LNA) colonel Ahmed Al Masmary claimed that 'branches of the Muslim Brotherhood affiliated to al-Qaeda' had joined forces with ISIS in Libya.

descriptions: Islamic State of Iraq is linked to al-Qaeda in Iraq.
edges.evidence_quote: In November 2010, the militant group Islamic State of Iraq, which is linked to al-Qaeda in Iraq, threatened to 'exterminate all Iraqi Christians'.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda in Iraq morphed into the Islamic State of Iraq after 2006.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda's Iraq branch, which later morphed into the Islamic State of Iraq after 2006

descriptions: AQAP and Islamic State-Yemen engaged in armed conflict over territory and interests.
edges.evidence_quote: a 'disagreement on properties, interests and positions' in July 2018 lead to a conflict between the two groups

descriptions: AQAP and ISIS-Y were in conflict but it ended by 2020.
edges.evidence_quote: AQAP's conflict with ISIS-Y had also dissipated in 2020, with no attacks being reported against either groups since February 2020.

descriptions: AQAP's media became more militaristic in response to the rise of the Islamic State, implying rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The rise of the Islamic State as well as the Houthis in 2014 led to an increase in militarism and sectarianism in AQAP's media.

descriptions: Al-Shabaab clashed violently with ISIL after rejecting its overtures.
edges.evidence_quote: The groups clashed violently on several occasions, most fiercely during 2015, and Al-Shabaab's internal security service continued to arrest, and sometimes execute, suspected ISIS supporters within Al-Shabaab's ranks.

descriptions: Al-Shabaab is in competition with Islamic State in Somalia.
edges.evidence_quote: possibly motivated by competition with Islamic State in Somalia

descriptions: Islamic State in Somalia is a splinter group from Al-Shabaab.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 2015, senior Al-Shabaab commander Abdul Qadir Mumin and approximately 20 of his followers in Puntland pledged allegiance to ISIL, establishing what became Islamic State in Somalia (ISIS).

descriptions: Badr Organization fought against ISIL in Iraq.
edges.evidence_quote: Badr Brigade forces, and their Iranian commanders, have come to prominence in 2014 fighting the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) in Iraq.

descriptions: Boko Haram and ISIL became enemies due to territorial disputes.
edges.evidence_quote: Boko Haram and ISIL were initially allies. However, the two groups became enemies due to territorial disputes, because ISIL tried to conquer the zones which were under Boko Haram's control.

descriptions: Boko Haram was formerly aligned with ISIL.
edges.evidence_quote: The group formerly aligned itself with the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant.

descriptions: Boko Haram pledged allegiance to ISIL and became its West African province.
edges.evidence_quote: In March 2015, Shekau pledged allegiance to ISIS self-styled caliph Abu Bakr Al-Baghdadi and became "West African Province" (Wilayat Garb Ifriqiyah), i.e. the West African province of the Islamic State.

descriptions: Boko Haram pledged allegiance to ISIL, becoming a province.
edges.evidence_quote: On 7 March, Boko Haram's leader Abubakar Shekau pledged allegiance to ISIL via an audio message posted on the organisation's Twitter account.

descriptions: Camorra may be in alliance with Islamic State for drug distribution.
edges.evidence_quote: According to numerous articles, the Camorra may be in alliance with the Islamic State .

descriptions: The Grey Wolves list Islamic State as an opponent.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Islamic State, Kurdistan Workers' Party, Kurdish Hezbollah, Raiders Organization

descriptions: The GTA gang was formed to train soldiers for ISIL.
edges.evidence_quote: Formed in March 2012 by Ibaydullo Subkhanov to train soldiers for the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL)

descriptions: Hezbollah fought against the Islamic State in Syria.
edges.evidence_quote: Hezbollah also fought against the Islamic State.

descriptions: Islamic State attacked Houthi worshippers, killing 142.
edges.evidence_quote: On 20 March the al-Badr and al-Hashoosh mosques came under suicide attack during midday prayers, and the Islamic State quickly claimed responsibility.

descriptions: IS lists Abu al-Fadl al-Abbas Forces as a non-state opponent, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Other non-state opponents: ... Abu al-Fadl al-Abbas Forces

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Ahrar al-Sham

descriptions: Ajnad al-Sham Islamic Union is listed as an opponent of Islamic State.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Ajnad al-Sham Islamic Union

descriptions: Al-Khansaa Brigade is the all-female media wing of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: the media wing of IS's all-female Al-Khansaa Brigade

descriptions: Al-Nusra Front is listed as an opponent of Islamic State.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Al-Nusra Front

descriptions: Al-Nusra and ISIL are hostile towards each other, with differing approaches to Druze.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Nusra and ISIL are both hostile towards the Druze. However, while al-Nusra has typically destroyed Druze shrines and pressured them to convert to Sunni Islam, ISIL regards the entire Druze community as a valid target for violence, as it does the Yazidis.

descriptions: ISIL and al-Nusra Front occasionally cooperate against the Syrian government.
edges.evidence_quote: ISIL and al-Nusra Front still cooperate with each other occasionally when they fight against the Syrian government.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda and Islamic State entered open conflict starting February 2014.
edges.evidence_quote: 3 February 2014 : Beginning of Al-Qaeda–Islamic State conflict

descriptions: Al-Qaeda cut ties with ISIL due to conflict over al-Nusra Front.
edges.evidence_quote: In February 2014, Ayman al-Zawahiri, the leader of Al-Qaeda, announced that his group Al-Qaeda had cut ties with the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant and denounced ISIL after being unable to reconcile a conflict between them and the al-Qaeda affiliate al-Nusra Front.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda disavowed relations with ISIL in February 2014, indicating a split.
edges.evidence_quote: In February 2014, after continued tensions, al-Qaeda publicly disavowed any relations with ISIL.

descriptions: Islamic State (as ISI) affiliated with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them.
edges.evidence_quote: the organisation (primarily under the Islamic State of Iraq name) affiliated itself with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them during the 2003–2006 phase of the Iraqi insurgency

descriptions: The Islamic State (as Islamic State of Iraq) was affiliated with al-Qaeda and fought alongside them.
edges.evidence_quote: the organisation (primarily under the Islamic State of Iraq name) affiliated itself with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them during the 2003–2006 phase of the Iraqi insurgency.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda provided financial support to JTJ, the precursor to IS.
edges.evidence_quote: The financial support from Al Qaeda came after Zarqawi was reluctant to be recruited by bin Laden.

descriptions: Islamic State of Iraq fought alongside al-Qaeda in Iraq during the insurgency.
edges.evidence_quote: the organisation (primarily under the Islamic State of Iraq name) affiliated itself with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them during the 2003–2006 phase of the Iraqi insurgency.

edges.evidence_quote: Other non-state opponents: al-Qaeda, al-Nusra Front, Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula, Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb, Al-Shabaab, Taliban, Hamas, Hezbollah, Houthis, Kurdistan Workers' Party, Syrian Democratic Forces, ...

descriptions: Islamic State regards Hamas as apostates and considers them opponents.
edges.evidence_quote: IS regards the Palestinian Sunni group Hamas as apostates who have no legitimate authority to lead jihad and see fighting Hamas as the first step towards confrontation by IS with Israel.

descriptions: IS-AP is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Azerbaijan Province (IS-AP) emerged in 2019 and operates in Azerbaijan. Its adeherents were previously recruited by both the Islamic State – Caucasus Province (IS-CP) and Islamic State – Khorasan Province (ISIS-K).

descriptions: IS-BP is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Bengal Province (IS-BP) is active in Bangladesh. The group is an offshoot of the Al-Qaeda affiliated Jamaat-ul-Mujahideen Bangladesh (JMB). It emerged in 2015 and was claimed by IS in 2016.

descriptions: IS-CP is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Caucasus Province (IS-CP) is active in the North Caucasus region of Russia. IS announced the group's formation in 2015.

descriptions: IS-CAP is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Central Africa Province (IS-CAP) first appeared in 2018 and operates in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda since 2020. It emerged out of the Ugandan Islamist group Allied Democratic Forces (ADF).

descriptions: ISHP is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Hind Province (ISHP) is the Indian branch of IS and operates in Indian Jammu and Kashmir. Its formation was announced in 2019.

descriptions: ISIS-K is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Khorasan Province (ISIS-K) emerged in 2015 and operates in Central and South Asia.

descriptions: ISPP is an official affiliate of IS, split from ISIS-K.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Pakistan Province (ISPP) was formed in 2019 as a split from Islamic State – Khorasan Province( ISIS-K).

descriptions: ISMP is an official affiliate of IS, formerly part of IS-CAP.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Mozambique Province (ISMP), formerly part of Islamic State – Central Africa Province (IS-CAP), was formed in 2022.

descriptions: ISPP is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Philippines Province (ISPP) operates in the Philippines, primarily in Mindanao. It emerged in 2016 from local jihadist groups Maute Group, Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters (BIFF), and Ansar Khalifa Philippines (AKP).

descriptions: ISSP is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Sahel Province (ISSP), formerly known as Islamic State in the Greater Sahara (IS-GS), operates mainly in Mali, Niger and Burkina Faso.

descriptions: ISSP is an official affiliate of IS, formed by defectors from Al-Shabaab.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Somalia Province (ISSP) operates primarily in Puntland in northern Somalia. The group first appeared in 2015 when elements within the Somali Islamist group Al-Shabaab defected and pledged allegiance to IS.

descriptions: IS-TP is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Turkey Province (IS-TP) was formed in 2019 and is active in Turkey.

descriptions: ISWAP is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – West Africa Province (ISWAP) is primarily active in the Chad Basin of Niger, Nigeria, Cameroon and Chad. It emerged in 2015 after a split from Boko Haram.

descriptions: Islamic State originated from Jaish al-Ta'ifa al-Mansurah.
edges.evidence_quote: Originating in the Jaish al-Ta'ifa al-Mansurah founded by Abu Omar al-Baghdadi in 2004

descriptions: The Islamic State originated from Jaish al-Ta'ifa al-Mansurah, which was its predecessor.
edges.evidence_quote: Originating in the Jaish al-Ta'ifa al-Mansurah founded by Abu Omar al-Baghdadi in 2004, the organisation (primarily under the Islamic State of Iraq name) affiliated itself with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them during the 2003–2006 phase of the Iraqi insurgency.

descriptions: Jaysh al-Islam criticized ISIL for killing Muslims.
edges.evidence_quote: The Jaysh al-Islam group within the Islamic Front criticised ISIL, saying: 'They killed the people of Islam and leave the idol worshippers ... They use the verses talking about the disbelievers and implement it on the Muslims'.

edges.evidence_quote: the main combatants were sectarian Shia and Sunni armed groups, such as the Islamic State of Iraq and the Mahdi Army

descriptions: Islamic State and Tahrir al-Sham were in conflict, resulting in the death of an IS leader.
edges.evidence_quote: the Islamic State claimed that al-Husseini al-Qurashi was killed in a shootout with Tahrir al-Sham (HTS) forces.

descriptions: Taliban banned association with IS's Khorasan branch, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: the Taliban banned all Afghans from associating with the local Khorasan Province branch of IS in July 2022, and labelled it a 'false sect'.

descriptions: ISIS–K is a regional branch of the Islamic State.
edges.evidence_quote: The Islamic State – Khorasan Province ( ISKP or ISIS–K ) is a regional branch of the Salafi jihadist group Islamic State (IS)

descriptions: ISIS–K is a regional branch of Islamic State.
edges.evidence_quote: ISIS–K is a regional branch of the Salafi jihadist group Islamic State (IS)

descriptions: ISS is an affiliate of the Islamic State.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Somalia Province ( IS–SP or ISS ) or Abnaa ul-Calipha or also just Islamic State of Somalia is an affiliate of the Islamic State

descriptions: ISS is an affiliate of the Islamic State, pledging allegiance to its caliph.
edges.evidence_quote: The group first appeared in the latter half of 2015 when pro-Islamic State fighters within al-Shabaab defected and pledged allegiance to IS caliph Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi.

descriptions: The PKK lists the Islamic State as an opponent.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Islamic State

descriptions: The PKK fought against ISIL in Syria and Iraq.
edges.evidence_quote: The PKK engaged the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) forces in Syria in mid-July 2014 as part of the Syrian Civil War.

descriptions: Islamic State fighters are increasing presence in Afghanistan, opposing Taliban.
edges.evidence_quote: Russian officials also called for action against Islamic State (IS) fighters, who Russia said have started to increase their presence in Afghanistan since the Taliban's takeover.

descriptions: The Islamic State provided funding to the Taliban for a short period.
edges.evidence_quote: About 54 percent of the funding came from foreign governments, 10 percent from private donors from abroad, and 16 percent from al-Qaeda and the Islamic State.

descriptions: Infobox lists Islamic State as an opponent of Wagner Group.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: List: Islamic State

descriptions: Wagner fought against ISIL in Syria.
edges.evidence_quote: He was also reported to had been involved in the first offensive to capture Palmyra from the Islamic State (ISIL) in early 2016.

descriptions: The two gangs were engaged in a violent feud with multiple shootings and murders.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2007, the feud between two street gangs from Croxteth and Norris Green reached its peak with the murder of innocent 11 year old Rhys Jones .

descriptions: The Crocky Crew and Strand Gang were engaged in a violent war that led to the death of an innocent child.
edges.evidence_quote: In August 2007, the ongoing war between two rival gangs the ‘Crocky Crew’ and ‘Strand Gang’, caused nationwide outrage when innocent 11-year-old Rhys Jones was shot in the back as he walked home from football practice and died in his mother's arms in the car park of the Fir Tree pub in the Croxteth district of Liverpool.

descriptions: The Amirante clan was allied with the Ferraiuolo-Stolder clan.
edges.evidence_quote: quest'ultimi alleati del clan Ferraiuolo-Stolder

descriptions: The Amirante clan was allied with the Ferraiuolo-Stolder clan.
edges.evidence_quote: quest'ultimi alleati del clan Ferraiuolo-Stolder

descriptions: The Paranza dei Bambini was allied with the Amirante clan.
edges.evidence_quote: affiancati dai clan Sibillo, Brunetti e Amirante

descriptions: The Paranza dei Bambini was allied with the Amirante clan.
edges.evidence_quote: afferenti a un cartello camorristico formato dai giovani della famiglia Giuliano, in conflitto con i loro stessi parenti da molti anni, affiancati dai clan Sibillo, Brunetti e Amirante

descriptions: Amirante was allied with Sibillo and Giuliano against Mazzarella.
edges.evidence_quote: affiancati dai clan Sibillo, Brunetti e Amirante, quest'ultimi alleati del clan Ferraiuolo-Stolder e appoggiati esternamente dal gruppo Rinaldi di San Giovanni a Teduccio

Whitney gang.description: The Whitney gang was a family-based criminal organization from the Anfield district of Liverpool, England, active from the 1980s until 2011. They were involved in drug trafficking, specifically conspiracy to supply Class A drugs. In November 2011, members and associates were sentenced to a total of 82 years in prison. The gang was led by the Whitney family, including Carol, Leslie, Lisa, Paul, Anthony, and Gary Whitney.

descriptions: Misso, Pirozzi, and Savarese families founded a criminal association together.
edges.evidence_quote: La nascita del sodalizio criminale tra le tre famiglie Misso, Pirozzi e Savarese sarebbe da datare intorno agli anni '80

5T.description: Vietnamese crime gang active in Cabramatta, Sydney, Australia from the mid-1980s to 1999. Formed by Vietnamese refugees, the gang engaged in drug trafficking and extortion. Its name derives from five Vietnamese words starting with T. The gang dissolved after the murder of its leader in 1995 and subsequent power struggles.

descriptions: D'Agostino acted as a link between Serraino-Condello-Imerti and Commisso-Cordì-Aquino.
edges.evidence_quote: l'anello di congiunzione tra le cosche reggine facenti capo ai Serraino-Condello-Imerti e quelle della Locride (Commisso-Cordì-Aquino)

31 Gang.description: A street gang based in East Buffalo, New York, founded in the early 2000s and named after the ID number of the Harriet Ross Tubman School. The gang operates in the Willert Park and Broadway-Fillmore areas, primarily involved in drug trafficking, including cocaine and crack cocaine. Members have faced federal charges, and the gang has been active in conflicts with law enforcement.

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham allied with Ajnad al Sham in Jund al Malahim operations room.
edges.evidence_quote: On 21 October 2015, the Jund al Malahim operations room was created as an alliance of Ajnad al Sham, Ahrar al-Sham and Al-Nusra in Rif Dimashq.

Savage Nomads.description: The Savage Nomads were a mostly Puerto Rican and African American street gang founded in the South Bronx, New York City in 1970 by Benjamin Melendez. Active primarily in the 1970s, the gang engaged in small crimes and battles with rival gangs such as the Seven Immortals and Savage Skulls. After a period of dormancy, the gang resurfaced in 2017 with homicides linked to them.

descriptions: The Ghetto Brothers and Savage Nomads merged to form Los Solidos in the Connecticut prison system during the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: the Ghetto Brothers and the Savage Nomads joined to form Los Solidos

descriptions: The Savage Nomads engaged in running battles with the Dirty Dozen.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was involved in a number of running battles with rival gangs Seven Immortals, Savage Skulls , and the Dirty Dozen.

descriptions: The Savage Nomads merged with Ghetto Brothers to form Los Solidos.
edges.evidence_quote: the Ghetto Brothers and the Savage Nomads joined to form Los Solidos

descriptions: The Savage Nomads engaged in running battles with the Savage Skulls.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was involved in a number of running battles with rival gangs Seven Immortals, Savage Skulls , and the Dirty Dozen.

descriptions: The Savage Nomads engaged in running battles with the Seven Immortals.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was involved in a number of running battles with rival gangs Seven Immortals, Savage Skulls , and the Dirty Dozen.

descriptions: Savage Skulls had running battles with Savage Nomads.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang ... was also involved in a number of running battles with rival gangs, including the Seven Immortals, Savage Nomads, and Dirty Dozen.

descriptions: FIIIR is a rival of AFCA.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: FIIIR

descriptions: Outlaws were recruiting Freeboarders as an affiliated club.
edges.evidence_quote: According to law enforcement, the Outlaws were in the process of recruiting the Freeboarders in order to gain a foothold in Cincinnati, which provoked retaliation from the Iron Horsemen.

Trafficante crime family.description: The Trafficante crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Tampa, Florida, founded around 1920 by Ignacio Antinori. It was most notably led by Santo Trafficante Jr., who was involved with the CIA in assassination plots against Fidel Castro. After his death in 1987, Vincent LoScalzo controlled the family until his death in 2025. The family is still active.

descriptions: Bonanno family is allied with Trafficante crime family per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Trafficante crime family

descriptions: Trafficante family was represented by the Chicago Outfit on the Commission.
edges.evidence_quote: Trafficante crime family (Tampa)

Colombo crime family.description: The Colombo crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the Five Families of New York City. Founded in 1928 by Joe Profaci, it is the youngest of the Five Families and has a history of instability and infighting, including three internal wars. The family engages in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, extortion, drug trafficking, and other criminal activities. It remains active, though weakened by leadership turnover and prosecutions.

descriptions: The Colombo crime family is listed as an ally of the 116th Street Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino , Lucchese , Purple Gang

descriptions: The Colombo family is a constituent family of the American Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: la familia criminal Colombo

descriptions: Colombo family held a seat on the Commission from 1931 to 1990s and again from 2000s.
edges.evidence_quote: Colombo (1931–1990s; 2000s–present)

descriptions: Bonanno capo Massino sought advice from Colombo boss Persico, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Massino turned to Colombo crime family boss Carmine Persico and Gambino crime family boss Paul Castellano for advice; they told him to act immediately.

descriptions: Maranzano family had a close relationship with Profaci family.
edges.evidence_quote: Maranzano was also close to Joseph Profaci, future boss of the New York Profaci family.

descriptions: Bonanno family was allied with Profaci family through personal and marriage ties.
edges.evidence_quote: Joseph became a powerful member of the Commission, owing to his close relationship with fellow boss Joe Profaci. The bond between the two became even stronger when Joseph's son Salvatore "Bill" Bonanno married Profaci's niece Rosalie in 1956. The Bonanno-Profaci alliance deterred the other three families from trying to move in on their rackets.

edges.evidence_quote: Magliocco joined a plot by Bonanno family boss Joseph Bonanno to assassinate the three other bosses on the Commission to take control of the New York Mafia.

descriptions: Bonanno conspired with Profaci (Colombo) boss Magliocco to assassinate Commission rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Joe Bonanno made plans to assassinate several rivals on the Commission—bosses Tommy Lucchese, Carlo Gambino, and Stefano Magaddino, as well as Frank DeSimone. Bonanno sought Profaci crime family boss Joseph Magliocco's support

edges.evidence_quote: In 2017, he promoted Canadian mobster Domenico Violi to Underboss of the family with the approval of the New York Bonanno , Genovese and Colombo families.

descriptions: Commission excluded Colombo family from leadership.
edges.evidence_quote: the Commission refused to allow any Colombo member to sit on the Commission and considered dissolving the family.

Luppino crime family.description: The Luppino crime family is an 'Ndrangheta organized crime family based in Hamilton, Ontario, Canada, founded in the 1950s by Giacomo Luppino. It is one of three centralized Mafia organizations in Hamilton, alongside the Musitano and Papalia families. The family has strong connections with the Buffalo crime family and has been involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, and other criminal activities. By 2018, the Violi family had increased its role, but both Violi brothers were imprisoned for drug offenses. The family is now believed to be the Buffalo family's Canadian crew.

descriptions: Mammoliti and Luppino are related.
edges.evidence_quote: I Mammoliti sono una 'ndrina potente di Oppido Mamertina e Castellace quali fanno capo i Rugolo , i Nava, i Cosoleto, i Luppino e i Romeo con loro imparentate.

descriptions: Paolo Violi's sons were affiliated with the Luppino 'ndrina in Canada.
edges.evidence_quote: Nei primi anni 2000, i due figli di Paolo Violi erano affiliati alla 'Ndrina Luppino a Hamilton (Canada)

descriptions: The Luppino family has strong connections with the Buffalo crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Luppinos have had strong connections with the Buffalo crime family of Buffalo, New York.

descriptions: The Luppinos have had strong connections with the Buffalo crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Luppinos have had strong connections with the Buffalo crime family of Buffalo, New York.

descriptions: The Luppino family is now under the control of the Buffalo crime family as its Canadian crew.
edges.evidence_quote: In recent times the Luppino crime family has fallen under the control of the Buffalo crime family and is now believed to be the Buffalo family's Canadian crew.

descriptions: Paolo Violi, associated with the Luppino family, developed connections with the Cotroni family.
edges.evidence_quote: In Montreal, Violi developed connections with the Cotroni crime family , while maintaining ties with the Luppino family

descriptions: Paolo Violi, a member of the Luppino-Violi family, joined the Cotroni family.
edges.evidence_quote: Paolo Violi (1931 - 1978), figlio di Domenico. Trasferitosi in Canada nel 1951, entra a fare parte della famiglia Cotroni.

descriptions: Hells Angels is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Independent Soldiers , Musitano crime family , Rizzuto crime family , 14K (triad) , Hells Angels

descriptions: Independent Soldiers is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Independent Soldiers , Musitano crime family , Rizzuto crime family , 14K (triad) , Hells Angels

descriptions: The Luppinos and Musitanos are involved in a mafia war in Hamilton.
edges.evidence_quote: one report stated that the Luppinos and the Musitano crime family seemed to be involved in the city's 'mafia war'.

descriptions: The Luppino family supported the anti-Rizzuto faction during a civil war, indicating conflict with the Rizzuto family.
edges.evidence_quote: During a civil war within the Rizzuto family in the early 2010s, both the Luppino and the Violi families, still angry over the murder of Paolo Violi, supported the anti-Rizzuto faction.

descriptions: The Luppino-Violi crime family is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Luppino-Violi crime family, Papalia crime family, Various gangs over Canada including their allies

descriptions: Musitano planned to murder Luppino family members to become godfather.
edges.evidence_quote: Murdock also claimed that he was waiting for Pat Musitano to approve the murders of four Luppino crime family members Natale Luppino and Vincenzo Luppino (the two sons of Giacomo Luppino) and Domenic Violi and Giuseppe Violi (the two sons of Paolo Violi).

descriptions: Luppino crime gang is an enemy of Musitano family.
edges.evidence_quote: The enemies included 'criminal groups in Hamilton, Buffalo, Montreal and elsewhere, including the Luppino and Papalia crime gangs,' according to the National Post.

descriptions: Infobox lists Luppino as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Luppino crime family

descriptions: Michele Racco, head of the Siderno Group, maintained close contacts with the Luppino crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: He maintained close contacts with the Luppino crime family in Hamilton, Ontario

descriptions: Kavač clan abducted the leader of Barska OCG, a constituent of rival Škaljari clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Sky ECC data has revealed that "Kavač" OCG was able to penetrate higher echelons of rival "Škaljari" OCG during 2020, by using sophisticated deception techniques. Particularly publicized case was abduction and subsequent months-long illegal detention of Mile Radulović, leader of "Barska" OCG.

descriptions: Barska OCG is a regional clan within Škaljari OCG.
edges.evidence_quote: it operates as an association of highly independent regional clans - "Barska," OCG, "Budvanska" OCG, "Zemunska / Bojović" OCG.
